# Draft the complete article

Use `brief.md`, `research.md`, `intent.md`, and `outline.md` to return a complete
`draft.md`.

Fulfill the alignment contract and section jobs in useful prose. Cite each
material factual claim to a source in the research ledger, preserve caveats,
and distinguish fact from interpretation. Use the primary topic naturally;
never repeat a phrase to satisfy an imagined density target. Keep headings,
examples, definitions, comparisons, and the call to action specific to the
reader. Do not expose provider credentials or raw private analytics.

End with `<!-- COMPLETE -->` only when the article is complete enough for
claim-level verification.
